heading: CLI Reference
text: CLI Documentation Complete reference for the inter-collect command-line tool.

heading: Commands
text: Command Description collect Download datasets from selected source(s) extract Decompress .gz files in parallel status Show collection progress and disk statistics tree Display the category/taxonomy tree rename Switch folder naming between display and code styles

heading: Global Flags
text: These flags are placed before the command name and apply to all sources. Flag Default Description --stats all Source: all , europe , uk , switzerland , unhcr , hdx -o, --output ./stats_data Root output directory -v, --verbose off Enable debug logging

heading: Source-Specific Flags
text: These flags are also placed before the command but only affect certain sources. They are silently ignored by other sources. Flag Sources Description --scope <ORG> Swiss, HDX Organization filter: slug or all --formats <FMT,...> Swiss, HDX Comma-separated data formats (e.g., csv,xls ) --year-from <YEAR> UNHCR Start year (default: 1951) --year-to <YEAR> UNHCR End year (default: latest)

heading: collect
text: Download all datasets from the selected source(s). inter-collect [--stats <SOURCE>] [source-flags] collect [collect-flags] Flag Default Description -c, --concurrency 5 Max parallel downloads (auto-capped for ONS=2, UNHCR=1) -d, --delay 0.5 Seconds between download batches --skip-existing on Skip already downloaded files --no-skip-existing — Re-download all files --retry-failed off Include previously failed datasets --filter-path <PATH> — Only download under this catalog path --filter-codes <A,B,

heading: extract
text: Decompress .tsv.gz and .sdmx.xml.gz files using parallel workers. Primarily used for Eurostat. inter-collect [--stats <SOURCE>] extract [extract-flags] Flag Default Description -c, --concurrency auto Worker count (default: 2x CPU cores, max 32) -b, --buffer 64 I/O buffer size in MB per worker --force off Re-extract already extracted files

heading: status
text: Show collection progress, state file summary, and disk statistics. inter-collect [--stats <SOURCE>] status No additional flags — uses -o to find the data directory.

heading: tree
text: Fetch and display the category/taxonomy tree from the data source. inter-collect [--stats <SOURCE>] tree [--depth <N>] Flag Default Description --depth 3 Maximum tree depth to display

heading: rename
text: Rename existing directories between naming styles. inter-collect [--stats <SOURCE>] rename <display|code> [--dry-run] Argument/Flag Description display Rename folders to human-readable slugified titles code Rename folders to raw statistical office codes --dry-run Preview what would be renamed without applying

heading: Flag Compatibility Matrix
text: Which source-specific flags apply to which sources: Flag Eurostat UK ONS Swiss UNHCR HDX --scope ✗ ✗ ✓ ✗ ✓ --formats ✗ ✗ ✓ ✗ ✓ --year-from ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓ ✗ --year-to ✗ ✗ ✗ ✓ ✗ --concurrency cap none 2 none 1 none --verify ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓

heading: Common Patterns
text: Download everything from all sources inter-collect collect Preview before downloading inter-collect --stats europe collect --dry-run Verify and fix incomplete downloads inter-collect --stats uk collect --verify Retry failed datasets inter-collect --stats switzerland collect --retry-failed Download specific datasets inter-collect --stats europe collect --filter-codes prc_hicp_midx,nama_10_gdp Swiss: specific organization, CSV only inter-collect --stats switzerland --scope bundesamt-fur-energie-bf

heading: Eurostat
text: Eurostat (EU) CLI flag: --stats europe · ~10,400 datasets · SDMX 2.1 Dissemination API · No authentication required

heading: API Endpoints
text: Base URL: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/api/dissemination Endpoint URL Purpose Catalogue /catalogue/toc/xml Full Table of Contents (XML) SDMX Data /sdmx/2.1/data/{code} Statistical data (TSV + SDMX XML) Datastructure /sdmx/2.1/datastructure/ESTAT/{code}/latest Data Structure Definition Dataflow /sdmx/2.1/dataflow/ESTAT/{code}/1.0 Dataflow with resolved codelists Content Constraint /sdmx/2.1/contentconstraint/ESTAT/{code}/latest Valid dimension values ESMS Metadata ec.europa.eu/eurostat/cache/met

heading: Flag Compatibility
text: Flag Supported Notes --scope ✗ Not applicable — Eurostat has a single source --formats ✗ Not applicable — always downloads TSV + SDMX + structural metadata --year-from / --year-to ✗ Not applicable — each dataset contains all available years --concurrency ✓ Default 5; safe to increase for Eurostat --verify ✓ Checks that TSV or SDMX data files exist on disk --dry-run ✓ Preview without downloading --retry-failed ✓ Retry datasets that failed in a previous run --filter-path ✓ Filter by catalog path (

heading: Quick Reference (All Flags)
text: # collect — all applicable flags for Eurostat inter-collect --stats europe collect \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <CONCURRENCY:int> \ -d <DELAY:seconds> \ --skip-existing | --no-skip-existing \ --retry-failed \ --filter-path <CATALOG_PATH:str> \ --filter-codes <CODE1,CODE2,...> \ --dry-run \ --verify \ --folder-style <display|code> \ -v # extract — decompress .tsv.gz and .sdmx.xml.gz files inter-collect --stats europe extract \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <WORKERS:int> \ -b <BUFFER_MB:int> \ --force \ -v # sta

heading: Download Datasets
text: # Download all ~10,400 datasets (default) inter-collect --stats europe collect # Preview what would be downloaded inter-collect --stats europe collect --dry-run # Download with higher concurrency inter-collect --stats europe collect --concurrency 10 # Download specific datasets only inter-collect --stats europe collect --filter-codes prc_hicp_midx,nama_10_gdp # Download only datasets under a category path inter-collect --stats europe collect --filter-path database_by_themes/economy # Retry previ

heading: Extract Compressed Files
text: Eurostat delivers data as .tsv.gz and .sdmx.xml.gz . After collection, extract them: # Extract all .gz files (auto-detects CPU cores for parallelism) inter-collect --stats europe extract # Extract with specific worker count and buffer size inter-collect --stats europe extract --concurrency 8 --buffer 128 # Re-extract already extracted files inter-collect --stats europe extract --force

heading: Check Collection Status
text: inter-collect --stats europe status

heading: View Category Tree
text: # Show category tree (default depth 3) inter-collect --stats europe tree # Show deeper nesting inter-collect --stats europe tree --depth 5

heading: Rename Folders
text: # Switch from display names to raw codes inter-collect --stats europe rename code --dry-run inter-collect --stats europe rename code # Switch back to display names inter-collect --stats europe rename display

heading: Files Per Dataset
text: For a dataset with code prc_hicp_midx , up to 9 files are created: File Format Description prc_hicp_midx.tsv.gz Gzip Raw TSV data (compressed) prc_hicp_midx.tsv TSV Extracted tab-separated data prc_hicp_midx.sdmx.xml.gz Gzip SDMX 2.1 XML data (compressed) prc_hicp_midx.sdmx.xml XML Extracted SDMX observations prc_hicp_midx_dsd.xml XML Data Structure Definition prc_hicp_midx_dataflow.xml XML Dataflow with resolved references prc_hicp_midx_metadata.html HTML ESMS reference metadata prc_hicp_midx_c

heading: Output Structure
text: stats_data/eurostat/ eurostat_tree_index.json .eurostat_state.json eurostat/ database_by_themes/ general_and_regional_statistics/ european_and_national_indicators/ balance_of_payments/ ei_bpm6ca_q/ ei_bpm6ca_q.tsv.gz ei_bpm6ca_q.tsv ei_bpm6ca_q.sdmx.xml.gz ...

heading: Disk Space
text: A full Eurostat collection requires approximately 150-200 GB : Component Size Compressed data ( .tsv.gz + .sdmx.xml.gz ) ~30-40 GB Extracted data ( .tsv + .sdmx.xml ) ~100-120 GB Structural metadata (DSD + Dataflow + Constraint) ~15-20 GB Reference metadata + Info JSON ~1-2 GB

heading: License
text: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) Term Details Commercial use Allowed Attribution Required — acknowledge Eurostat as the source Legal basis Commission Decision 2011/833/EU Full terms ec.europa.eu/eurostat/help/copyright-notice

heading: UK ONS
text: UK ONS (Office for National Statistics) CLI flag: --stats uk · ~337 datasets · ONS CMD API (Beta) · No authentication required

heading: API Endpoints
text: Base URL: https://api.beta.ons.gov.uk/v1 Endpoint URL Purpose Dataset listing GET /datasets Paginated list of all datasets Dataset detail GET /datasets/{id} Full metadata Edition listing GET /datasets/{id}/editions Editions (e.g., "time-series") Version detail GET /datasets/{id}/editions/{ed}/versions/{ver} Download URLs, dimensions Downloads download.ons.gov.uk CSV, XLSX, and CSV-W files

heading: Rate Limiting
text: The ONS API has no documented hard rate limit but returns 429 Too Many Requests under moderate load. The collector handles this with: Sequential batched version resolution (5 per batch, 1s between requests, 2s between batches) Exponential backoff on 429: 20s, 40s, 80s, 160s, 320s, 640s Retry-After header support Concurrency capped at 2

heading: Flag Compatibility
text: Flag Supported Notes --scope ✗ Not applicable — ONS is a single source --formats ✗ Not applicable — always downloads CSV + XLSX + CSV-W --year-from / --year-to ✗ Not applicable — each dataset contains all available years --concurrency ✓ Auto-capped at 2 (ONS rate limits aggressively) --verify ✓ Checks that CSV/XLSX/CSV-W files exist on disk (not just metadata) --dry-run ✓ Preview without downloading --retry-failed ✓ Retry datasets that failed due to 429 errors --filter-path ✓ Filter by taxonomy

heading: Quick Reference (All Flags)
text: # collect — all applicable flags for UK ONS inter-collect --stats uk collect \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <CONCURRENCY:int> \ -d <DELAY:seconds> \ --skip-existing | --no-skip-existing \ --retry-failed \ --filter-path <TAXONOMY_PATH:str> \ --filter-codes <CODE1,CODE2,...> \ --dry-run \ --verify \ --folder-style <display|code> \ -v # status — show collection progress inter-collect --stats uk status -o <OUTPUT_DIR> # tree — display category hierarchy inter-collect --stats uk tree --depth <DEPTH:int> -v #

heading: Download Datasets
text: # Download all ~337 ONS datasets inter-collect --stats uk collect # Preview what would be downloaded inter-collect --stats uk collect --dry-run # Download specific datasets inter-collect --stats uk collect --filter-codes cpih01,mm23,lms # Retry previously failed datasets (common after 429 rate limiting) inter-collect --stats uk collect --retry-failed # Verify completed datasets have actual data files (not just info/meta JSON) # This catches datasets that were marked complete but only have metada

heading: Check Collection Status
text: inter-collect --stats uk status

heading: View Category Tree
text: inter-collect --stats uk tree inter-collect --stats uk tree --depth 4

heading: Rename Folders
text: inter-collect --stats uk rename code --dry-run inter-collect --stats uk rename display

heading: Files Per Dataset
text: For a dataset with code cpih01 , up to 5 files are created: File Format Description cpih01_info.json JSON Catalog snapshot cpih01_meta.json JSON Full version metadata (dimensions, release info) cpih01.csv CSV Data in CSV format cpih01.xlsx XLSX Data in Excel format cpih01.csv-metadata.json JSON CSV-W metadata (data dictionary)

heading: Output Structure
text: stats_data/ons/ ons_tree_index.json .ons_state.json uk_ons/ economy_inflation_priceindices/ cpih01/ cpih01.csv cpih01.xlsx cpih01.csv-metadata.json cpih01_meta.json cpih01_info.json peoplepopulation_community_wellbeing/ ... Datasets are organised into taxonomy-based category folders.

heading: License
text: Open Government Licence v3.0 (OGL v3.0) Term Details Commercial use Allowed Attribution Required — "Source: Office for National Statistics licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0" Compatibility Compatible with CC BY 4.0

heading: Swiss FSO
text: Swiss FSO (opendata.swiss) CLI flag: --stats switzerland · ~1,977 (BFS) / ~6,699 (all orgs) datasets · opendata.swiss CKAN API · No authentication required

heading: API Endpoints
text: Base URL: https://ckan.opendata.swiss/api/3/action/ Endpoint URL Purpose Package Search GET /api/3/action/package_search Paginated dataset search Package Show GET /api/3/action/package_show Single dataset detail Organization List GET /api/3/action/organization_list 288 government organizations Group List GET /api/3/action/group_list 14 thematic categories

heading: Flag Compatibility
text: Flag Supported Notes --scope ✓ Organization filter: slug or all (default: BFS) --formats ✓ Comma-separated formats (default: csv,xls,ods,json) --year-from / --year-to ✗ Not applicable — UNHCR only --concurrency ✓ Default 5; no aggressive rate limiting --verify ✓ Checks that data files (CSV/XLS/ODS/JSON) exist on disk --dry-run ✓ Preview without downloading --retry-failed ✓ Retry previously failed datasets --filter-path ✓ Filter by group path --filter-codes ✓ Filter by CKAN package names --folder

heading: Quick Reference (All Flags)
text: # collect — all applicable flags for Swiss FSO inter-collect --stats switzerland \ --scope <ORG_SLUG:str|all> \ --formats <csv,xls,ods,json> \ collect \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <CONCURRENCY:int> \ -d <DELAY:seconds> \ --skip-existing | --no-skip-existing \ --retry-failed \ --filter-path <GROUP_PATH:str> \ --filter-codes <PACKAGE1,PACKAGE2,...> \ --dry-run \ --verify \ --folder-style <display|code> \ -v # status — show collection progress inter-collect --stats switzerland status -o <OUTPUT_DIR> # tr

heading: --scope (Organization Filter)
text: Use --scope to select which organization's datasets to download. Accepts any opendata.swiss organization slug. (default) : BFS only — bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs (~3,277 datasets) --scope all : All 288 organizations (~6,700+ datasets with downloadable resources) --scope ORG_SLUG : Any specific organization Browse all Swiss organizations to find the right scope value. Swiss Federal Agencies Scope Value Organization Datasets bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs Federal Statistical Office (BFS) (default) 3,

heading: --formats
text: Default: csv,xls,ods,json (all downloadable data formats) Example: --formats csv for CSV only (~308 datasets) Skips HTML, SERVICE, PDF, and other non-data resources automatically

heading: Download Datasets
text: # Download all BFS datasets, all formats (default) inter-collect --stats switzerland collect # Preview what would be downloaded inter-collect --stats switzerland collect --dry-run # Verify completed datasets have actual data files, re-download if missing inter-collect --stats switzerland collect --verify # CSV only inter-collect --stats switzerland --formats csv collect # All organizations inter-collect --stats switzerland --scope all collect # Specific org: swisstopo inter-collect --stats switz

heading: Check Collection Status
text: inter-collect --stats switzerland status

heading: View Category Tree
text: inter-collect --stats switzerland tree inter-collect --stats switzerland tree --depth 4

heading: Rename Folders
text: inter-collect --stats switzerland rename code --dry-run inter-collect --stats switzerland rename display

heading: Files Per Dataset
text: File Format Description {code}_info.json JSON Catalog snapshot (organization, license, resource URLs) {code}.csv CSV Data in CSV format (if available) {code}.xls Excel Data in Excel format (if available) {code}.ods ODS Data in ODS format (if available) {code}.json JSON Data in JSON format (if available) When multiple resources of the same format exist in different languages, filenames include a language suffix (e.g., dataset_de.csv , dataset_fr.csv ).

heading: Output Structure
text: stats_data/swiss/ swiss_tree_index.json .swiss_state.json swiss_open_data/ agriculture_fisheries_forestry_food/ rohholzbilanz3/ rohholzbilanz3_info.json rohholzbilanz3.csv rohholzbilanz3.xls population_society/ erwerbstatige-inlandkonzept/ erwerbstatige-inlandkonzept_info.json erwerbstatige-inlandkonzept.csv erwerbstatige-inlandkonzept.xls

heading: License
text: Opendata.swiss Terms of Use Term Details Commercial use Varies by dataset — some allow free commercial reuse, others require prior authorisation Attribution Always required BFS Terms bfs.admin.ch terms of use

heading: UNHCR
text: UNHCR Refugee Statistics CLI flag: --stats unhcr · ~450 datasets (6 endpoints x 75 years) · UNHCR Population Statistics API · No authentication required

heading: API Endpoints
text: Base URL: https://api.unhcr.org/population/v1 Endpoint Path Data Population /population/ Stock figures: refugees, asylum-seekers, IDPs, stateless Asylum Applications /asylum-applications/ Individual asylum applications by country Asylum Decisions /asylum-decisions/ Decisions: recognized, rejected, closed Solutions /solutions/ Durable solutions: return, resettlement, naturalisation Demographics /demographics/ Age/gender breakdown of displaced populations UNRWA /unrwa/ UNRWA-registered Palestine r

heading: Flag Compatibility
text: Flag Supported Notes --scope ✗ Not applicable — UNHCR has a single fixed API --formats ✗ Not applicable — always downloads JSON data --year-from ✓ Start year filter (default: 1951) --year-to ✓ End year filter (default: latest available) --concurrency ✓ Auto-capped at 1 (UNHCR rate limits aggressively) --verify ✓ Checks that _data.json files exist on disk --dry-run ✓ Preview without downloading --retry-failed ✓ Retry datasets that failed due to 403/429 errors --filter-path ✓ Filter by endpoint pa

heading: Quick Reference (All Flags)
text: # collect — all applicable flags for UNHCR inter-collect --stats unhcr \ --year-from <START_YEAR:int> \ --year-to <END_YEAR:int> \ collect \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <CONCURRENCY:int> \ -d <DELAY:seconds> \ --skip-existing | --no-skip-existing \ --retry-failed \ --filter-path <ENDPOINT_PATH:str> \ --filter-codes <ENDPOINT_YEAR1,ENDPOINT_YEAR2,...> \ --dry-run \ --verify \ --folder-style <display|code> \ -v # status — show collection progress inter-collect --stats unhcr status -o <OUTPUT_DIR> # tree

heading: Year Filtering (--year-from / --year-to)
text: # All years (default: 1951-present, ~450 datasets) inter-collect --stats unhcr collect # Recent data only inter-collect --stats unhcr --year-from 2020 --year-to 2023 collect # From a year onward inter-collect --stats unhcr --year-from 2015 collect # Single year inter-collect --stats unhcr --year-from 2023 --year-to 2023 collect

heading: Download Datasets
text: # Download all endpoints, all years inter-collect --stats unhcr collect # Preview what would be downloaded inter-collect --stats unhcr collect --dry-run # Download specific endpoint+year combinations inter-collect --stats unhcr collect --filter-codes population_2023,demographics_2023 # Retry previously failed datasets inter-collect --stats unhcr collect --retry-failed # Verify completed datasets have data files inter-collect --stats unhcr collect --verify Important: UNHCR concurrency is automati

heading: Check Collection Status
text: inter-collect --stats unhcr status

heading: View Category Tree
text: inter-collect --stats unhcr tree inter-collect --stats unhcr tree --depth 3

heading: Rename Folders
text: inter-collect --stats unhcr rename code --dry-run inter-collect --stats unhcr rename display

heading: Files Per Dataset
text: For each endpoint+year combination (e.g., population_2023 ), 2 files are created: File Format Description population_2023_info.json JSON Catalog snapshot (endpoint, year, page count) population_2023_data.json JSON Complete paginated data with all rows The _data.json file contains all rows across all API pages, merged into a single JSON array with aggregate totals.

heading: Rate Limiting
text: The UNHCR API has undocumented rate limits. The collector uses: Sequential requests (concurrency: 1) 2-second delay between requests Exponential backoff on 429/5xx: 15s, 30s, 60s, 120s

heading: Output Structure
text: stats_data/unhcr/ unhcr_tree_index.json .unhcr_state.json unhcr/ population/ population_2023/ population_2023_info.json population_2023_data.json asylum_applications/ asylum-applications_2023/ ... demographics/ ...

heading: License
text: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) Term Details Commercial use Allowed Attribution Required — "UNHCR Refugee Population Statistics Database" Full terms UNHCR Terms of Use for Datasets

heading: HDX
text: HDX (Humanitarian Data Exchange) CLI flag: --stats hdx · ~244 (UNHCR) / ~19,000+ (all orgs) datasets · HDX CKAN API · No authentication required for reads

heading: About HDX
text: The Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX) is OCHA's open platform for sharing humanitarian data across crises and organisations. It is built on CKAN and hosts data from 400+ organizations including UN agencies, NGOs, governments, and research institutions. HDX complements the UNHCR Statistics API by providing per-country refugee population CSVs, situation reports, border crossing data, camp locations, and voluntary repatriation statistics — the country-level breakdown data that the UNHCR Statistics AP

heading: Major Organizations on HDX
text: Organization Datasets Focus Area World Bank Group 4,792 Development indicators, economics, poverty Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team 2,612 Geospatial / mapping data UNHCR (default scope) 1,137 Refugees, asylum-seekers, IDPs, stateless FEWS NET 847 Food security & early warning HeiGIT 785 Geoinformatics for humanitarian action WHO 683 Health, disease outbreaks, epidemiology WFP 492 Food assistance, nutrition, logistics Copernicus 478 Satellite / earth observation FAO 441 Agriculture, food systems,

heading: API Endpoints
text: Base URL: https://data.humdata.org/api/3/action/ Endpoint URL Purpose Package Search GET /api/3/action/package_search Paginated dataset search Package Show GET /api/3/action/package_show Full metadata and download links

heading: Flag Compatibility
text: Flag Supported Notes --scope ✓ Organization filter: slug or all (default: UNHCR) --formats ✓ Comma-separated formats (default: csv,xlsx,xls) --year-from / --year-to ✗ Not applicable — UNHCR source only --concurrency ✓ Default 5; HDX has no documented rate limits --verify ✓ Checks that data files (CSV/XLSX/XLS) exist on disk --dry-run ✓ Preview without downloading --retry-failed ✓ Retry previously failed datasets --filter-path ✓ Filter by group path --filter-codes ✓ Filter by CKAN package names -

heading: Quick Reference (All Flags)
text: # collect — all applicable flags for HDX inter-collect --stats hdx \ --scope <ORG_SLUG:str|all> \ --formats <csv,xlsx,xls> \ collect \ -o <OUTPUT_DIR> \ -c <CONCURRENCY:int> \ -d <DELAY:seconds> \ --skip-existing | --no-skip-existing \ --retry-failed \ --filter-path <GROUP_PATH:str> \ --filter-codes <PACKAGE1,PACKAGE2,...> \ --dry-run \ --verify \ --folder-style <display|code> \ -v # status — show collection progress inter-collect --stats hdx status -o <OUTPUT_DIR> # tree — display category hier

heading: --scope (Organization Filter)
text: Use --scope to select which organization's datasets to download. Accepts any CKAN organization slug. (default) : UNHCR datasets only (~244 with downloadable CSV/XLS) --scope all : All 364+ organizations (~16,390 datasets) --scope ORG_SLUG : Any specific organization Browse all HDX organizations to find the right scope value. UN Agencies & Major International Organizations Scope Value Organization Datasets unhcr UNHCR - The UN Refugee Agency (default) 1,137 world-health-organization World Health

heading: --formats
text: Default: csv,xlsx,xls (all downloadable data formats) Skips PDF, Web App, KMZ, and other non-data resources

heading: Download Datasets
text: # Download all UNHCR datasets (default) inter-collect --stats hdx collect # Preview what would be downloaded inter-collect --stats hdx collect --dry-run # Verify completed datasets have actual data files, re-download if missing inter-collect --stats hdx collect --verify # World Food Programme inter-collect --stats hdx --scope wfp collect # International Organization for Migration inter-collect --stats hdx --scope international-organization-for-migration collect # World Health Organization, CSV o

heading: Check Collection Status
text: inter-collect --stats hdx status

heading: View Category Tree
text: inter-collect --stats hdx tree inter-collect --stats hdx tree --depth 3

heading: Rename Folders
text: inter-collect --stats hdx rename code --dry-run inter-collect --stats hdx rename display

heading: Files Per Dataset
text: File Format Description {code}_info.json JSON Catalog snapshot (organization, license, resource URLs) {code}.csv CSV Data file (if available) {code}.xlsx Excel Data file (if available) {code}.xls Excel (legacy) Data file (if available) When multiple resources share the same format, filenames use the sanitised resource name.

heading: Output Structure
text: stats_data/hdx/ hdx_tree_index.json .hdx_state.json hdx/ refugees/ unhcr-population-data-for-afg/ unhcr-population-data-for-afg_info.json unhcr-population-data-for-afg.csv asylum_seekers/ ...

heading: License
text: Varies per dataset. Common licenses on HDX: License Description CC BY-IGO Most UNHCR data — share/adapt with attribution CC BY Share/adapt for any purpose with attribution CC BY-SA Share-alike — derivatives must use same license ODC-ODbL Database-specific — attribution + share-alike CC0 Public domain — no restrictions Check each dataset's metadata for its specific license. The _info.json file includes license_url and license_title fields.
